title: linaria virgata subsp. calycina
intro: Linaria virgata subsp. calycina, also known as Toadflax, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Linaria virgata subsp. calycina include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, Toadflax is often used as a decoction or infusion, with the dried flowers and leaves being steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
